qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via workflow import-from-url endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes OS command injection in the Git node (CWE-78). This finding describes SSRF via GET /rest/workflows/from-url with code locations in packages/cli/src/workflows/workflows.controller.ts and the import-workflow-from-url DTO. The vulnerable component (workflow import-from-url endpoint vs Git node), root cause (missing URL validation causing SSRF vs command injection), and vulnerability class (SSRF vs CWE-78 command injection) are all different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-9g95-qf3f-ggrw) describes OS command injection in the n8n Git node (CWE-78), where authenticated users with workflow creation/editing permissions can execute arbitrary system commands or read arbitrary files on the n8n host. The only qualifying finding (ssrf-workflow-import-url) describes SSRF in GET /rest/workflows/from-url — a distinct vulnerable component with a different root cause from the advisory-described Git node command injection.
evidence: Advisory summary explicitly states: 'OS Command Injection in Git Node' and references CWE-78 (OS Command Injection).

Advisory description states: 'Vulnerabilities in the Git node allowed authenticated users with permission to create or modify workflows to execute arbitrary system commands or read arbitrary files on the n8n host.'

The 'ssrf-workflow-import-url' finding targets GET /rest/workflows/from-url with code locations in packages/cli/src/workflows/workflows.controller.ts:321 and the import-workflow-from-url DTO — not the Git node.
The finding does not reference the Git node, git-related operations, or command injection through git command construction.
